extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper timed his sprint perfectly inside final 100m after BOIVIN Guillaume crash at 4.3km fractured peloton and eliminated favorites
tactical_patterns: VANMARCKE Sep attacked 23km out targeting Golden Kilometer bonus seconds, revealing positioning value before circuit compression begins
BOIVIN Guillaume crash 4.3km out split peloton; being ahead of late-race chaos worth more than pure sprint power
PHILIPSEN Jasper waited until VAN POPPEL Danny committed first inside 100m, using opponent's leadout as position reference point

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: superior sprint timing discipline, won while favorites crashed out, also banked Golden Kilometer bonus seconds
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: neutral: strong enough to initiate final sprint but became positioning foil for smarter finisher
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: had confident leadout but still only third, suggests hesitation or poor wheel choice in compressed finale
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: second place from chaotic sprint shows consistent positioning skill even without pure speed advantage
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: aggressively contested Golden Kilometer bonuses despite not contending sprint, tactical awareness intact
rider_signals.VAN HOOYDONCK Nathan: negative: biggest crash casualty, positioning failure cost both stage and classics preparation
